latin: Galileus perspicillum suum ad caelum direxit et lunas Iovis vidit. quattuor corpora parva circa planetam magnum movebant. 'non omnia circa terram moventur,' scripsit, et mundus mutatus est.

questions.prompt: What did Galileo see through his telescope?

questions.target: It proved not everything orbits the Earth
choices: It proved not everything orbits the Earth
It showed Jupiter was larger than thought
It disproved the existence of stars

translation: Galileo pointed his telescope at the sky and saw the moons of Jupiter. Four small bodies were moving around the great planet. 'Not everything moves around the Earth,' he wrote, and the world was changed.
